result.chapter_summary: The author argues that the American educational decline and current political malaise stem from a shift away from Enlightenment-based 'Common Schools,' which emphasized reason and shared factual knowledge, toward Romantic child-centered models that trust in 'natural development.' He asserts that recent cognitive science vindicates the Founders' skepticism of human nature and their belief that a stable democracy requires citizens to be unified through a common, knowledge-rich curriculum.

claims.text: Patriotism was intense in both American political parties during the late 1950s.
claims.source_passage: The late '50s was a very different America. Patriotism was intense in both political parties.

claims.text: The idea that human history is inherently progressive, as taught by Hegel and Dewey, is now passé.
claims.source_passage: This book is partly devoted to showing that Hegel and Dewey are passé—along with the idea that human history is inherently progressive.

claims.text: National reading scores in the United States began showing a significant decline in the late 1940s.
claims.source_passage: Take for example our national reading scores. They started showing a big decline in the late 1940s.

claims.text: The creators of the American political system were skeptical about human nature.
claims.source_passage: In prior decades, when our average literacy was higher, the USA had flourished because the creators of our political system were skeptical about human nature.

claims.text: The Founders did not believe that following 'nature' was a sound principle for the Common Schools.
claims.source_passage: They did not think that following nature was a good idea in our Common Schools.

claims.text: Human nature, when followed without check, leads to injustice and bloodshed.
claims.source_passage: They were especially wary of following human nature, which, as they knew from history, can lead to injustice and bloodshed.

claims.text: The Common School required the attendance of students from all social classes.
claims.source_passage: The "common school" required common attendance by all social classes.

claims.text: Students in Common Schools were intended to study a shared curriculum of science, moral tales, and history.
claims.source_passage: Students would study in common the same science, and moral tales, and the same cautionary warnings from history about human nature, its imperfections and its unreliability and villainies.

claims.text: The aim of the Common School was to produce 'literate, ethical patriots' in whom reason and science dominated over passion and rhetoric.
claims.source_passage: The Founders wanted to create citizens in whom reason and science and empathetic moral rectitude would dominate over passion and rhetoric... The aim of the common school was to form literate, ethical patriots.

claims.text: To retain ultimate power, the citizenry must be well educated factually, historically, and ethically.
claims.source_passage: We the people were collectively to retain ultimate power. Hence we the people must be well educated factually, historically, and ethically.

claims.text: Romanticism replaced the Enlightenment faith in reason with a trust in instincts and strong emotions as 'natural and holy.'
claims.source_passage: The new ideas proposed that we must no longer rely on mere reason. Our instincts and our strong emotions could be trusted. They were natural and holy.

claims.text: The concept of 'natural development' in child-centered education is rooted in Romantic philosophy.
claims.source_passage: Nature and the "child-centered" theme of "natural development" were to dominate in education.

claims.text: John Dewey's progressive educational theories were influenced by the philosophy of Hegel.
claims.source_passage: Hegel's concern... So also believed Dewey, father of our child-centered "progressive" education. He once conceded that Hegel had left a "permanent deposit" in his mind.

claims.text: Recent cognitive science confirms that the educational views of the Enlightenment Founders were correct and Romantic child-centered theories are wrong.
claims.source_passage: This book is based on recent cognitive science showing that our Enlightenment Founders were right and that the child-centered romantics who now dominate our early education have been deeply wrong.

claims.text: The adoption of child-centered educational theories is a primary cause of current American political hostility and slogan-dominated discourse.
claims.source_passage: Their mistakes have caused much of the "malaise" that we currently experience in our hostile, slogan-dominated politics.

claims.text: The Romantic movement prioritized nature over history as the primary guide for education.
claims.source_passage: Nature, not history, was to be the new, God-governed guide in education.

claims.text: Hegel and Schelling's philosophy opposed the perceived 'narrowness' of mere reason in favor of the forward march of history.
claims.source_passage: In Germany, Hegel and the friend of his youth, Schelling, wrote imposing philosophical tomes against the 'narrowness' of mere reason. Never mind the insights of past history. The forward march of future history was Hegel's concern.

claims.text: John Dewey's child-centered progressive education was fundamentally influenced by Hegelian philosophy.
claims.source_passage: So also believed Dewey, father of our child-centered 'progressive' education. He once conceded that Hegel had left a 'permanent deposit' in his mind.

claims.text: Romanticism in educational policy results in confusion, disunion, and illiteracy.
claims.source_passage: In educational policy, romanticism has brought us confusion, disunion, and illiteracy.

claims.text: American fifteen-year-olds rank significantly low—25th—internationally in literacy, math, and science.
claims.source_passage: Our fifteen-year-olds rank 25th among the nations in combined literacy, math, and science.

claims.text: Romantic educational theories unwittingly promoted racism by essentializing racial and ethnic differences.
claims.source_passage: The romantics unwittingly also brought us racism by sentimentalizing and essentializing human differences of race and ethnicity.

claims.text: Core Knowledge curriculum provides a cumulative buildup of shared knowledge without compromising inclusivity or local relevance.
claims.source_passage: This cumulative approach results in high grades in state reading tests while still honoring students' home cultures and enabling them to be included.

claims.text: Child-centrism is itself a Eurocentric theory that is empirically incorrect.
claims.source_passage: educators who continue to object to the 'Eurocentrism' of Core Knowledge should be reminded that child-centrism is not only a Eurocentric theory; it is also an incorrect theory.

claims.text: European nations that adopt child-centered theories are also declining in international educational rankings.
claims.source_passage: That's why *several* European nations are falling behind, along with the USA, on the international PISA tests for fifteen-year-olds.

claims.text: Empathy is a cognitive component at the heart of literacy.
claims.source_passage: The cognitive science described in this book (some of it very new) will show that *empathy* lies at the heart of literacy itself.

claims.text: Literacy is the essential requirement for sustaining a democratic society.
claims.source_passage: And literacy is the indispensable agency for sustaining democracy.

claims.text: The theories of 'reading levels' and 'Lexiles' are scientifically incorrect and were created to facilitate the Romantic ideal of individualized choice.
claims.source_passage: To enable that individualized choice of readings, romantic child-centrists in charge of our education schools created the scientifically incorrect theory of generalized 'reading levels,' and they created unsound reading measures called 'Lexiles.'

claims.text: Child-centered progressivism fails because it lacks a concrete curriculum.
claims.source_passage: our chance to turn away from romantic child-centered progressivism, which lacked a concrete curriculum.

claims.text: The moral flaw of child-centered education is encouraging children to choose their own readings based on their natural instincts.
claims.source_passage: Al thus called out the moral flaw at the heart of child-centered education—which is to encourage the child herself to choose (by nature's guiding hand) her own readings from the classroom library.

claims.text: Lexiles are unsound measures of reading ability.
claims.source_passage: and they created unsound reading measures called 'Lexiles.'

claims.text: Current literacy scores in the United States reflect the literacy levels of parents rather than the actual abilities of the children.
claims.source_passage: Our children's literacy scores currently tend to reflect the literacy levels of their parents rather than the abilities of the child.

claims.text: Social predestination in educational outcomes does not occur in shared knowledge 'common schools.'
claims.source_passage: Such social predestination does not occur in shared knowledge schools such as the 'common schools' that our founders initiated.

claims.text: Child-centered educational practices have caused a multi-decade decline in American reading scores.
claims.source_passage: With some admirable exceptions, current practices have caused a multi-decade decline in our reading scores and in the malnourishment of potential talent.

claims.text: The decline in American reading scores preceded school desegregation.
claims.source_passage: But the decline preceded school desegregation and tragically impeded the effectiveness of desegregation.

claims.text: The lack of shared knowledge instruction impeded the effectiveness of school desegregation.
claims.source_passage: But the decline preceded school desegregation and tragically impeded the effectiveness of desegregation. For, under the misguided reign of child-chosen readings, disadvantaged children of all colors have failed to overcome their home literacy disadvantage.

claims.text: Disadvantaged children require commonality of reading instruction rather than individuality of instruction to succeed.
claims.source_passage: Hence commonality of reading instruction was required—not individuality of instruction.

claims.text: Statewide curriculum frameworks and assessment systems are necessary for students and teachers to understand learning responsibilities.
claims.source_passage: What we really need—at the very least—are statewide curriculum frameworks and statewide assessment systems. Then, students and teachers in every school will know what kids are responsible for learning and whether they have learned it.

claims.text: The first state to adopt a statewide curriculum framework will become the national leader in literacy.
claims.source_passage: This book will explain why the first state to follow such a principle will jump to the top in literacy and become a change agent for the rest of the nation.

claims.text: Child-centered public schools hold back poverty-struck children by failing to follow a specific curriculum framework.
claims.source_passage: Jeff's students were poverty-struck multi-racial, multi-ethnic children who were being held back because the child-centered New York public schools were not following any specific curriculum framework.

claims.text: The rubber-stamped 'VETERAN' status on transcripts in the late 1950s was intended by institutions like Yale to signal that the candidate deserved a 'hard, respectful look' regarding their qualifications.
claims.source_passage: Yale wanted to say to the reader of the transcript: 'Whatever else you may want to know about the qualifications of this candidate, you should also know that he served and therefore deserves a hard, respectful look.'

claims.text: The European Enlightenment aimed to replace dogma with experience and logic as the primary drivers of knowledge.
claims.source_passage: the great intellectual movement that aimed to replace dogma with experience and logic.

claims.text: Romantics believed that human history is inherently progressive, a notion that serves as the foundation for child-centered progressive education.
claims.source_passage: They also said that human history is progressive-a confident notion that underlay child-centered 'progressive education.'

claims.text: John Dewey explicitly conceded that the philosophy of Hegel left a 'permanent deposit' in his mind.
claims.source_passage: He once conceded that Hegel had left a 'permanent deposit' in his mind.

claims.text: Romanticism should be restricted to the domain of poetry and excluded from the domain of educational policy.
claims.source_passage: Romanticism makes superb poetry, and that is where it should remain. In educational policy, romanticism has brought us confusion, disunion, and illiteracy.

claims.text: Core Knowledge schools are significantly oversubscribed by parents from diverse ethnic backgrounds.
claims.source_passage: So popular is this approach among diverse groups that Core Knowledge schools are hugely oversubscribed by parents of diverse home ethnicities.

claims.text: Normalized written English is highly stable and remains durable across generations.
claims.source_passage: Normalized written English has been so durable that our written language is, like all national print languages, highly stable and intergenerational.

claims.text: Education should foster citizens who possess empathy for 'human bad luck' as a means of sustaining democracy.
claims.source_passage: Americans ought to be skeptical of human nature and that our citizens should... sustain empathy for human bad luck.

claims.text: The argument that reading score declines were caused by school desegregation is a false narrative that transforms an 'educational vice into self-praising virtue.'
claims.source_passage: It's whispered that the decline was owing to school desegregation-thus transforming educational vice into self-praising virtue. But the decline preceded school desegregation and tragically impeded the effectiveness of desegregation.

claims.text: All impoverished eighth-grade students at Jeff Litt's initial Core Knowledge school were accepted into top high schools and subsequently received college fellowships.
claims.source_passage: After nine years in your K-8 school, all your impoverished, disadvantaged eighth graders were accepted by top high schools. From there they went off to college with fellowships.

evidence.description: Yale University graduate transcript containing a 'VETERAN' stamp to indicate the student's service record to potential reviewers.
National reading score data showing a decline starting in the late 1940s.
John Dewey's admission that Hegel's philosophy left a permanent influence on his thinking.
The historical institution of the separation of powers by the American Founders to mitigate the flaws of human nature.
PISA rankings showing US 15-year-olds rank 25th in combined literacy, math, and science.
International PISA test data indicating a decline in European nations using child-centered models.
High oversubscription rates of Core Knowledge schools by parents from diverse ethnic backgrounds.
Cognitive science research (unspecified in this section but referenced as 'very new') linking empathy to literacy.
The success of Jeff Litt's South Bronx K-8 school using the Core Knowledge Sequence, where all disadvantaged eighth graders were accepted to top high schools and colleges.
The replication of Jeff Litt's results in six additional charter schools funded by Carl Icahn.
Psycholinguistic findings regarding the necessity of shared background knowledge for spoken and written speech.
Historical data showing that the decline in reading scores started before the implementation of school desegregation.

concepts.author_definition: A school intended for all social classes that provides a shared, factual curriculum (science, history, and moral tales) to create a unified citizenry capable of reason.
A child-centered educational theme based on Romantic notions that education should follow the child's innate instincts rather than a structured history-based curriculum.
Citizens who possess the shared knowledge, reason, and moral rectitude necessary to maintain a democracy and resist political passion.
A Hegelian term meaning 'abolished' or 'superseded,' used here to describe how modern education views patriotism as outdated.
A cumulative buildup of shared knowledge and vocabulary designed to prepare students for mastery of future topics while remaining inclusive of diverse cultures.
An educational theory, characterized as Eurocentric and incorrect, that encourages children to choose their own readings based on innate instincts rather than a structured curriculum.
concepts.common_alternative: Progressive education that respects student agency.

concepts.author_definition: Scientifically unsound reading measures created to facilitate individualized reading choice in Romantic education models.
A scientifically incorrect theory used by romantic child-centrists to facilitate individualized reading choices rather than common content.
concepts.common_alternative: A tool for matching a student's current skill level to appropriate text difficulty.
concepts.source_passage: To enable that individualized choice of readings, romantic child-centrists in charge of our education schools created the scientifically incorrect theory of generalized 'reading levels'

concepts.author_definition: A condition in which a child's educational outcomes are determined by their parents' literacy and socio-economic status rather than school instruction.
An explicit, cumulative, and common set of learning objectives that students and teachers are held responsible for.
concepts.source_passage: Then, students and teachers in every school will know what kids are responsible for learning and whether they have learned it.

concepts.author_definition: The enduring and foundational influence of Hegelian thought on John Dewey's mind and educational theories.
Circumstances beyond an individual's control that require empathy from citizens to sustain a democratic society.
The rhetorical tactic of blaming poor educational outcomes on social progress (like desegregation) to avoid admitting the failure of educational methods.
The specific goal of the Common School, combining functional literacy with the moral rectitude and reason necessary for democratic citizenship.

cases.name: Mid-20th Century America (Late 1950s)
cases.description: A period characterized by intense, bipartisan patriotism following WWII and during the Korean War, where military service was highly valued in academic and professional qualifications.
key_passages: The late '50s was a very different America. Patriotism was intense in both political parties. World War II was recently over. But the Korean War followed-during which I had served.

Yale wanted to say to the reader of the transcript: 'Whatever else you may want to know about the qualifications of this candidate, you should also know that he served and therefore deserves a hard, respectful look.'

cases.name: The American Founding/Enlightenment Era
cases.description: The era when the US political system and Common Schools were established based on Enlightenment principles, logic, experience, and a deep skepticism of raw human nature.
key_passages: In prior decades, when our average literacy was higher, the USA had flourished because the creators of our political system were skeptical about human nature.

In short, by a lucky accident, both our nation and our common schools had been founded at the very height of the European Enlightenment-the great intellectual movement that aimed to replace dogma with experience and logic.

cases.name: US Education Post-Romantic Shift
cases.description: The transition of American schools toward child-centered progressivism led to a decline in international rankings (25th in PISA) and increased political polarization.
key_passages: In educational policy, romanticism has brought us confusion, disunion, and illiteracy. Our fifteen-year-olds rank 25th among the nations in combined literacy, math, and science.

cases.name: European PISA Decline
cases.description: Several European nations are falling behind in international assessments alongside the US due to their adoption of incorrect child-centered theories.
key_passages: That's why *several* European nations are falling behind, along with the USA, on the international PISA tests for fifteen-year-olds.

cases.name: South Bronx Core Knowledge School
cases.description: A school in the poorest section of New York City led by Jeff Litt. It implemented the Core Knowledge Sequence (common curriculum framework) for poverty-struck, multi-racial students. After nine years, every eighth grader was accepted to top high schools and went on to college with fellowships.
key_passages: About twenty-five years ago Jeff Litt was the principal of a school in the poorest section of New York City-the South Bronx. Jeff admired the Founders' Common School idea as exemplified by schools that were then using the Core Knowledge Sequence... Jeff's students were poverty-struck multi-racial, multi-ethnic children who were being held back because the child-centered New York public schools were not following any specific curriculum framework. Jeff, your first Core Knowledge school was a miraculous success.

cases.name: Icahn Charter Schools
cases.description: Six charter schools established by philanthropist Carl Icahn that followed Jeff Litt's model, achieving similar successful results with disadvantaged students.
key_passages: The philanthropist Carl Icahn bought you six more charter-school buildings with six more schools with similar results.

thinkers.name: John Dewey
thinkers.key_idea: The father of American child-centered 'progressive' education who integrated Hegelian ideas of historical progress into educational theory.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch links Dewey directly to the Romantic rejection of Enlightenment reason and argues his theories contributed to the current educational decline.
key_passages: I will offer several remarks about the unfortunate influence of Hegel on John Dewey-the father of American child-centered education.
So also believed Dewey, father of our child-centered "progressive" education. He once conceded that Hegel had left a "permanent deposit" in his mind.

thinkers.name: G.W.F. Hegel
thinkers.key_idea: The philosopher who proposed that human history is a march of progress and whose dense dialectical style influenced early 20th-century educational thought.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch characterizes Hegel's idea of inherently progressive history as a mistake that misled American education.
key_passages: Hegel and John Dewey have taught us that human history is a march of progress.
The forward march of future history was Hegel's concern. He explained that history was inherently marching forward.

thinkers.name: Alexander Pope
thinkers.key_idea: Enlightenment poet who stated 'The proper study of mankind is man.'
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Pope to signal the Enlightenment focus on studying human nature and history rather than following 'natural' instincts.
key_passages: Know then thyself; presume not God to scan; The proper study of mankind is man.

thinkers.name: William Wordsworth
thinkers.key_idea: Nature and the 'heart' are superior to science, art, and 'barren leaves' (books/history) as guides for human development.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Wordsworth's poetry to characterize the anti-intellectual, nature-focused roots of Romantic education.
key_passages: Enough of Science and of Art; / Close up those barren leaves; / Come forth and bring with you a heart / That watches and receives.

thinkers.name: Albert Shanker
thinkers.key_idea: Opposition to child-centered education due to its harm to poor and immigrant children and its lack of concrete curriculum.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch views Shanker as a 'literate patriot' and a critical ally who used his political power to challenge progressive educational dogmas.
key_passages: Shanker picked up the argument and the revolutionary cause... 'Where's the curriculum?' Shanker asked.

thinkers.name: Jeff Litt
thinkers.key_idea: Implementing an explicit, cumulative common curriculum (the Core Knowledge Sequence) can overcome the disadvantages of poverty in an urban school setting.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Litt's success in the South Bronx as a living proof of his theoretical claims about common-knowledge schooling.
key_passages: Jeff, your first Core Knowledge school was a miraculous success.
You put into practice what Al Shanker was demanding of all our public schools.

actors.name: American Founders
actors.context: The creators of the US political system and the Common School model who relied on Enlightenment reason.

actors.name: Yale University
actors.context: The institution where the author studied Hegel and which stamped transcripts with 'VETERAN' in the late 1950s.

actors.name: Core Knowledge Foundation
actors.context: The organization founded by Hirsch to implement cumulative, shared-knowledge curricula.

actors.name: Jeffrey Litt
actors.context: A New York educator whom Hirsch credits with helping keep schools on an egalitarian course.

actors.name: American Federation of Teachers (AFT)
actors.context: The union led by Albert Shanker that Hirsch briefly worked with to reform curricula.

actors.name: Albert Shanker
actors.context: Former president of the AFT and advocate for curriculum standards.

actors.name: Jeff Litt
actors.context: School principal in the South Bronx who implemented Core Knowledge.

actors.name: Carl Icahn
actors.context: Philanthropist who funded charter schools based on the common-knowledge model.

actors.name: American Federation of Teachers (AFT)
actors.context: The union through which Shanker promoted curriculum reform.

objections_raised.objection: Patriotism is outdated ('passé' or 'aufgehoben') because human history is a march of progress.
objections_raised.response: Hegel and Dewey themselves are passé, and history has shown that the Founders' skepticism of human nature was more accurate than Romantic optimism.

objections_raised.objection: The Core Knowledge school-reform effort is Eurocentric and lacks inclusiveness.
objections_raised.response: The curriculum has been updated to include more non-Eurocentric and local materials; furthermore, child-centrism is itself a Eurocentric and scientifically incorrect theory.

objections_raised.objection: The multi-decade decline in American reading scores was caused by school desegregation.
objections_raised.response: The decline actually preceded school desegregation. Furthermore, the lack of a shared curriculum (child-centered pedagogy) was what actually caused desegregation to be less effective for disadvantaged children.

cross_chapter_refs.reference_text: Chapter 1 offers data on the oversubscription of Core Knowledge schools by diverse groups.
See Appendix I for PISA test data showing US and European decline.
